text:
## Assessment: REVISE

### Blocking issue

- **Parallel conflict resolution has a result-bearing data race for incompatible claim orderings.**  
  `sembla_resolve_conflicts` runs one thread per candidate (`crates/sembla-cuda/src/backend.rs:513-536`, generated at `crates/sembla-cuda/src/codegen.rs:949`). When enabled candidates claim the same resource with incompatible ordering modes, every involved thread writes directly to shared `status[0..2]` (`codegen.rs:1007-1012`) without atomics or unique error slots. A valid model using, for example, race-time ordering in one transition and key ordering in another can reach this path. The resulting diagnostic fields—and formally the writes themselves—are nondeterministic, violating the Level A and exact CPU-oracle contracts. Move compatibility detection into the serial `sembla_validate_claims` phase or write per-candidate errors followed by deterministic reduction before parallel argmin.

### Acceptance criteria

| Criterion | Result |
|---|---|
| 1. Toolkit-free workspace build/test | **Pass** — locked workspace build and tests pass; deterministic codegen and updated SIR golden run everywhere. |
| 2. Device Philox equality | **Unanswered as permitted** — real ignored device test is compiled and discoverable. |
| 3. Required oracle-equality cases | **Unanswered as permitted** — SIR 100k/200, `sir_policy`, and canonical tests exist. |
| 4. Level A repeatability | **Unanswered as permitted** — repeat-run GPU test exists. |
| 5. No-device diagnostic | **Pass** — production `CUDA_ERROR_NO_DEVICE` mapping and frozen diagnostic are non-ignored tests. |
| 6. Honest reporting | **Pass** — `GPU-STATUS.md` records 2–4 as unanswered and links the provenance-producing remote runner. |

The prior blockers concerning two-part reductions, checked multiplication, eager claim/effect/filter evaluation, destination-ordered effects, and prospective-state wire aggregates are otherwise addressed.

Verification passed:

- `cargo build --workspace --locked`
- `cargo test --workspace --locked`
- CUDA-feature all-target check
- CUDA-feature clippy with `-D warnings`
- GPU test inventory
- Remote-runner shell validation
- `git diff --check`

```json
{"assessment":"REVISE","blockingIssues":["Parallel conflict-resolution threads write the shared status tuple concurrently when incompatible claim orderings meet on one resource; move this error detection to the serial validation phase or deterministically reduce per-candidate errors before argmin."],"summary":"All six acceptance-criterion surfaces are implemented or honestly marked unanswered, and prior revision blockers are resolved, but one reachable shared-status race remains in incompatible claim-order handling, so the generic Level A backend is not yet deterministic for every validated model."}
